sections.paragraph:
σμυρνίζω (s. σμύρνα; in the sense ‘be like myrrh’ Diosc., Mat. Med. 1, 66, 1 W.) perf. pass. ptc. ἐσμυρνισμένος treat with myrrh (cp. Cyranides p. 89, 13; 97, 20; PGM 36, 313; Cos. and Dam. 33, 115) ἐσμυρνισμένος οἶνος wine flavored with myrrh Mk 15:23 (cp. Pliny, NH 14, 13 vina myrrhae odore condita; Chariton 8, 1, 12 οἶνος κ. μύρα). The μυρσινίτης οἶνος (Diosc. 5, 37; Chion, Ep. 6), wine mixed with myrtle juice, was something different.—DELG s.v. σμύρνη. TW.

Diosc Diosc , or Dioscurides, I a.d. physician, also Ps.-Dioscorides after I a.d.—List 5

perf. perf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Cyranides Cyranides , a compilation relating to magical properties of stones, plants, and animals; perh. antedating I/II a.d.—List 5

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

Chariton Chariton , scriptor eroticus (writer of romances) I–II a.d.—List 5

Chion Chion , IV b.c.; for letters attributed to him, I a.d., s. Ep.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 933
